headline: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: XSS is an injection attack where malicious scripts are injected into trusted websites, executing in victims' browsers. Three types: Stored (persistent, in database), Reflected (in URL parameters), DOM-based (client-side JavaScript vulnerability). Prevention: output encoding, Content Security Policy (CSP), input validation.

citation.name: Web Security for Developers: Real Threats, Practical Defense (2025)
